title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes24_0e71776fed.html
content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY SEPTEMBER 2001 24th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY SEPTEMBER 2001 24th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY SEPTEMBER 2001 24th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY SEPTEMBER 2001 24th MONTHLY SUPPLEMENT Items to be deleted before or on October 15 2001 If you are likely to need to refer to any of the orders to be deleted before or on October 15 2001 you can still access the rules as they were at the 23rd Update by downloading them as a file in zipped format [link to /cpr_web/update.htm]. Items which come into force on October 15 2001 Please note that the amendments enclosed with this update (save for the very few exceptions listed below) do not come into force until October 15 2001. The changes involve not only the insertion of new text, but also remove a number of RSC and CCR Orders either completely or in part. Items which come into force before October 15 2001 Amendments to rules 16 and 17 of RSC Order 77 and rule 15 of CCR Order 49 came into force on March 26 2001 , but were omitted in error from the previous update. Amendments to RSC Order 112 and CCR Order 47 replacing references to ‘blood tests’ with ‘scientific tests’ are amendments which came into force on April 1 2001 but were omitted in error from the 22nd update. Amendments to existing material are included as follows: Rules Part 6 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 6.1 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK cross references amended to refer to new Part 55. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 6.16 the following cross references at the end of the Rule have been deleted: para (a) THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK O.10, O.97, O.113 ; para (b) THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK O.7, O.43, O.49 . THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 6.18 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK para (dd) [which is an inadvertent duplication of (ea)] is deleted. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 6.24 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK was printed twice – the first entry (which is incorrect) is deleted . Part 8 Rule 8.2A(2) ‘caes’ corrected to ‘cases’. Part 16 Rule 16.8 the following cross references at the end of the rule have been deleted: O.88, O.97, O.6, O.43 . Part 19 Rule 19.9(3)(a) ‘except’ is deleted . Part 22 Rule 22.1(1) new text replaces the existing text . Part 48 Rule 48.7 sub-paras 4 , 5 , 6 and 7 renumbered as 3 , 4 , 5 and 6 . Part 49 Para (2)(f), the word ‘and’ deleted ; para (2)(g) deleted. Part 51 The heading is amended to ‘Transitional Arrangements and Pilot Schemes’. Rule 51 is renumbered 51.1. . After Rule 51.1 a new Rule 51.2 has been inserted. Part 57 Explanatory note at end deleted (originally included in error). Schedule 1 RSC Order 10 revoked from October 15 2001. RSC Order 15 Rule 14(2) words ‘under Rule 13’ deleted and replaced by ‘under CPR Rule 19.7’. RSC Order 45 Rule 3 – ‘mortgage proceedings to which Order 88 applies’ deleted and new text inserted after ‘made in’. New para (2A) inserted. In para 3 ‘as is referred to in paragraph (2)’ inserted after ‘permission’. RSC Order 51 New title and Rule A1 inserted before existing Rule 1. Rule 2: ‘District judge’ added to those who may make an order to appoint a receiver. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK RSC Order 62 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Para 1(2) of Part II of Appendix 3 ‘Order 45, rule 3(2)’ substituted for ‘Order 88, rule 1 (mortgage claims)’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK RSC Order 77 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 16 para 2B ‘of the Post Office’ deleted. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 17 para (1) references to ‘Post Office Act 1969’ amended to ‘section 92 of the Postal Services Act 2000’. RSC Order 88 Rules 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 and 7 revoked from October 15 2001. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK RSC Order 93 Rules THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK 15 and THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK20 revoked from October 15 2001. RSC Order 97 Order revoked from October 15 2001. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK RSC Order 112 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK‘blood tests’ is replaced by ‘scientific tests’ wherever it appears . RSC Order 113 Rules 1 , 1A , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 and 8 revoked from October 15 2001. Rule 7 para 1 ‘under this order’ is amended to ‘in a possession claim against trespassers under Part 55’ . Schedule 2 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK CCR Order 4 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 3(a) revoked from October 15 2001. CCR Order 6 Rules 3, 5 and 5A revoked from October 15 2001. CCR Order 7 Order revoked from October 15 2001. CCR Order 22 Rule 8 para 1A sub-para (a) new text substituted for existing text. CCR Order 24 Rules 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , and 7 revoked from October 15 2001. Rule 6, para 1 ‘in a possession claim against trespassers under Part 55’ substituted for ‘under this order’. Rule 9 ‘a possession claim against trespassers under Part 55’ substituted for ‘proceedings for possession under Part I of this Order’. Rule 10 para (1) ‘Part 55’ substituted for ‘rules 2 to 7’. Rule 12 para (9)(c) ; Rule 13 para (4) and Rule 14(3)(c) ‘Part 55’ substituted for ‘Part 1 of this Order’. CCR Order 25 Rule 13 para (1) – new para substituted. Rule 13 para (2) – new text: ‘or the person against whom the possession order was made’ inserted after ‘debtor’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK CCR Order 38 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Appendix B Part III table, para 10 ‘Section II of CPR Part 55 (Possession claims)’ is substituted for ‘rule 6 or 6A of Order 49’ . CCR Order 43 Order to be revoked from October 15 2001. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK CCR Order 47 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK‘blood tests’ is replaced by ‘scientific tests’ wherever it appears. CCR Order 49 Rules 1 , 1A , 2 , 4 , 6A , 8 , 9 , 13 and 16 revoked from October 15 2001. Rule 6B words ‘and powers of arrest’ deleted from title . Rule 6B after para 7 new para 7A inserted ; new para 8 substituted for existing para ; new paras 8A , 8B and 8C inserted; new paras 9 , 10 and 11 substituted for existing paras. New paras 11A and 11B inserted. Rule 15 amended to substitute ‘Postal services Act 2000’ for ‘Post Office Act 1969’ in the heading , and ‘section 92 of the Postal Services Act 200’ for ‘section 30(5) of the Post Offices Act 1969’ in para 1 ; in para 2 ‘universal service provider’ is substituted for ‘Post Office’ . Practice Direction 16 Para 6 is deleted and the remaining paras renumbered accordingly. Practice Direction 23 Two consecutive paras are numbered 6.1; second reference renumbered 6.1A. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 35 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Para 4.2 – new text substituted for existing text. Practice Direction 39 Heading before para 8: space inserted in title between words ‘authorities’ and ‘human’. Practice Direction 49 Revoked with effect from October 15 2001 Practice Direction 52 Para 1.1 Section III moved to separate line (typesetting error). Para 5.8A: the word ‘and’ is inserted after sub-para (1) and deleted after sub-para (2) ; sub-para (3) is omitted . After para 5.8B new paras 5.8C and 5.8D inserted. Para 8.2 table ‘Midland and Oxford Circuit’ amended to ‘Midland Circuit’ ; ‘Oxford’ removed from Hearing Only Centres for Midland Circuit and placed in the provincial list for South Eastern Circuit. Paras 8.9(2) and 8.10 – ‘section 9(1)’ is amended to read ‘section 9’. Practice Direction 55 New para 1.6 inserted. Practice Direction 56 Para 1.1 sub-para (2) definition of ‘the 1985 Act’ added . Newpara 3.8 inserted after para 3.7 . Para 4.2 reference to s47(5) amended to s37(5) . Para 5.5 ‘immediately’ inserted after ‘the defendant must’ . New paras 5.8 and 5.9 inserted after existing para 5.7 . New paras 12.2 and 12.3 inserted after para 12.1 . Other Practice Directions THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Schedule 2 CCR Order 49 Practice Direction THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK New Practice Direction inserted. Insolvency Practice Direction New text and draft orders inserted after para 1.5. Pre-Action Protocols Clinical Disputes Telephone number of Secretary amended. Professional Negligence Word ‘draft’ deleted. Forms Note the following forms included with this update but which will not come into force until October 15 2001. N2 Claim form (probate claim). N2A Probate Form – notes for claimant. N2B Probate Claim – notes for defendant. N3 Acknowledgement of service (probate Claim). N205D Notice of issue (probate claim). The following is a list of new or revised forms included with the 23rd update (and reprinted here for convenience) but which will not come into force until October 15 2001. N206B Notice of issue (possession claim) a new form which does not replace N206. N5 Claim form for possession of property – a new single claim form to be used for starting a claim in all possession cases, except forfeiture. N5A Claim form for relief against forfeiture – new form. N7 Notes for defendant (mortgaged residential premises). N7A Notes for defendant (rented residential premises). N7B Notes for defendant (forfeiture of the lease). N119 Particulars of claim (rented residential premises) – revised version of existing form. N120 Particulars of claim (mortgaged residential premise
